title: Fireworks AI API keys should not be disclosed
description: Fireworks AI API keys provide authentication to the Fireworks AI inference platform, which hosts open-source large language models (LLMs) and generative AI services. When hardcoded in source code, these credentials can be exposed in version control systems, logs, or deployment artifacts. Unauthorized access to Fireworks AI API keys allows attackers to use your AI inference quota, access model endpoints, generate content at your expense, or exhaust your usage limits. Trust boundaries are violated when API keys are exposed in source code repositories or uncontrolled deployment environments.
recommendation: Never hardcode Fireworks AI API keys in source code. Store them securely using environment variables (FIREWORKS_API_KEY), secret management systems (AWS Secrets Manager, Azure Key Vault, HashiCorp Vault), or secure configuration services. Use .env files with proper .gitignore rules for local development. Implement key rotation policies and monitor API key usage for suspicious or unexpected activity.
impact: Exposed Fireworks AI API keys can lead to unauthorized access to AI inference services, financial costs from unauthorized model usage, quota exhaustion, data exposure from hosted models, and service abuse.

compliant.code:
import os
import requests

# Load API key from environment variable
api_key = os.environ.get('FIREWORKS_API_KEY')
if not api_key:
    raise ValueError('FIREWORKS_API_KEY environment variable not set')

headers = {'Authorization': f'Bearer {api_key}'}
response = requests.post('https://api.fireworks.ai/inference/v1/chat/completions', headers=headers)
compliant.description: API key loaded from environment variable

compliant.code:
import boto3
import json

def get_fireworks_api_key():
    """Retrieve Fireworks AI API key from AWS Secrets Manager."""
    client = boto3.client('secretsmanager')
    response = client.get_secret_value(SecretId='prod/fireworks/api-key')
    secret = json.loads(response['SecretString'])
    return secret['api_key']

api_key = get_fireworks_api_key()
compliant.description: Retrieving API key from AWS Secrets Manager

compliant.code:
# config.yaml
fireworks:
  # API key should be set via environment variable FIREWORKS_API_KEY
  api_key: ${FIREWORKS_API_KEY}
  base_url: https://api.fireworks.ai/inference/v1
  model: accounts/fireworks/models/llama-v3p1-70b-instruct
